body_txt: Who? An actor or musician, or anyone with a particular role in a tight organization, is all set up to go ahead with the next big project. Then something happens where they drop out, voluntarily or otherwise , and someone else takes over that position. The project then become exceptionally famous and those associated with it become popular and wealthy, including the person who joined at the last minute. They become the one everyone remembers, while the original member is left obscure except as a trivia item. Named for Pete Best, the first drummer of The Beatles , who was replaced by Ringo Starr just before they hit it big, with Ringo having suddenly become available after leaving another band. In films this tends to happen with The Other Marty , an actor is swapped out with scenes reshot, out only to see the film become a major hit. Same with a Deleted Role . In television this is often used with The Other Darrin and Suspiciously Similar Substitute , with Dropped After the Pilot and Second Episode Introduction particularly known for changing cast members just as a show is getting started. If the guy is in the band when they are famous, but all but forgotten, that person's Stuck in Their Shadow . If the guy never had a chance of becoming famous anyway, that's Breakup Breakout . If the band tries to avoid talking about the guy in the event he is brought up or tries to take down any uploaded footage of the period he was with them, he is an Old Shame . If their successor ends up more remembered, it is a case of More Popular Replacement . As a final thought, notice that in Real Life this trope is quite common since music groups or any team whose future success is not clear normally go through a series of line-up changes. NOTE: Please add only sentient beings to the Real Life folder. Compare Iconic Sequel Character . Contrast Lesser Star .
